description: State machines and identity events (backlog #18 coverage). Deviation (C.3b): investigating -> pending -> approved | withdrawn; terminal states are terminal - re-litigation is a NEW Deviation. Finding (C.3): open -> in-remediation -> closed; closed is terminal (dispositions are Deviations, not extra states); direct open -> closed is legal (finding resolved without remediation phase). Identity (D2): canonical-alias = same content, substitution allowed; replaces = lineage, substitution NEVER automatic. Relationship composition (C.5 floor): equal chains; supplements never chains; everything else degrades to the honest floor (supports).

deviation-transitions.reason: terminal is terminal - re-litigation is a new Deviation with its own record

finding-transitions.reason: closed is terminal; a recurrence is a new Finding

identity-events.reason: identity equivalence, authority-asserted rebrand of the SAME content
a revision is a different object with history - auto-substituting silently corrupts implementations, findings, attestations, mappings, tailorings (P7-B4)

relationship-composition.reason: only equal chains at full strength (C.5)
the conformance FLOOR: everything but equal-chains degrades honestly toward supports; a richer table is Chapter 8's, and a tool may implement it, but MUST NOT claim stronger than the floor without it
supplements is an attachment claim, not a crosswalk claim - it never chains (C.5); OLIR exports may down-translate the EDGE to supports, but no composed claim exists
floor only - conflict propagation needs Chapter 8's table, never the floor rule

shape-disjointness.name: each-type-matches-exactly-one
shape-disjointness.note: the nine canonical minimal objects below must each match exactly ONE kernel shape; the tenth (garbage) must match ZERO - shape-disjointness is a conformance property because objects carry no type discriminator
